---
title: "KGE par station vs superficie de drainage"
format: html
jupyter: meandre
---

```{python}
import os, subprocess
os.chdir(subprocess.check_output(
    ["git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel"], text=True
).strip())

import tomllib
import torch
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

with open("notebooks/slso/config/slso.toml", "rb") as f:
    cfg = tomllib.load(f)
```

## Chargement des données et du modèle

```{python}
from meandre.data.basin_cache import BasinCache
from meandre.model import HydroModel

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
cache = BasinCache(cfg["paths"]["basin_db"])
hydro = cache.load(device=device)

# Observations
obs = cache.load_observations(
    date_start=cfg["temporal"]["date_start"],
    date_end=cfg["temporal"]["date_end"],
    min_valid_days=365,
)

# Forçage (depuis le cache NetCDF local)
from meandre.data.gridded_forcing import extract_forcing
from pathlib import Path

forcing = extract_forcing(
    zarr_path=cfg["paths"]["weather_grid"],
    node_coords=hydro["node_coords"].cpu().numpy(),
    node_elev=None,
    date_start=cfg["temporal"]["date_start"],
    date_end=cfg["temporal"]["date_end"],
    cache_nc=Path(cfg["paths"]["forcing_cache"]),
    device=device,
)

print(f"Nodes: {hydro['n_nodes']}, Stations: {obs['n_stations']}")
print(f"Device: {device}")
```

```{python}
# Construire le modèle et charger le checkpoint
from meandre.routing.graph import RiverGraph

graph = hydro["graph"]
n_nodes = hydro["n_nodes"]

model = HydroModel(
    n_nodes=n_nodes,
    n_forcing=cfg["model"]["n_forcing"],
    context_window=cfg["model"]["context_window"],
    residual_history=cfg["model"]["residual_history"],
    max_travel_time=cfg["model"]["max_travel_days"],
    dropout=cfg["model"]["dropout"],
    concrete_dropout=cfg["model"].get("concrete_dropout", False),
    concrete_init_p=cfg["model"].get("concrete_init_p", 0.1),
    param_mode=cfg["model"].get("param_mode", "nerf"),
).to(device)

ckpt = cfg["paths"]["checkpoint"]
model.load(ckpt)
model.eval()

# Curriculum: disable modules not yet trained
# Residual corrector activates at epoch 20 — checkpoint is pre-epoch-20
model.use_residual = False
# Travel-time attention disabled (epoch 9999)
if hasattr(model.routing, "use_tta"):
    model.routing.use_tta = False

print(f"Checkpoint: {ckpt}")
print(f"use_temporal={model.use_temporal}, use_residual={model.use_residual}")
```

## Simulation sur la période de validation

```{python}
from meandre.utils.state import HydroState

territorial = hydro["territorial"]
withdrawals = cache.load_withdrawals(
    date_start=cfg["temporal"]["date_start"],
    date_end=cfg["temporal"]["date_end"],
    device=device,
)

# Dates
dates = pd.date_range(cfg["temporal"]["date_start"], cfg["temporal"]["date_end"], freq="D")
day_of_year = torch.tensor([d.timetuple().tm_yday for d in dates], device=device)

# Indices temporels
spinup_end = (pd.Timestamp(cfg["temporal"]["spinup_end"]) - pd.Timestamp(cfg["temporal"]["date_start"])).days + 1
val_start_idx = (pd.Timestamp(cfg["temporal"]["val_start"]) - pd.Timestamp(cfg["temporal"]["date_start"])).days
val_end_idx = (pd.Timestamp(cfg["temporal"]["val_end"]) - pd.Timestamp(cfg["temporal"]["date_start"])).days + 1

print(f"Spinup: 0:{spinup_end}")
print(f"Val: {val_start_idx}:{val_end_idx} ({val_end_idx - val_start_idx} days)")
```

```{python}
# Simulation continue : spinup -> train -> val (pas de saut!)
# Le modèle a besoin de l'état accumulé sur 2017-2022 pour bien simuler 2023-2024.
with torch.no_grad():
    # Phase 1: spinup (2016) depuis état froid
    _, spun_state = model.simulate(
        forcing=forcing[:spinup_end],
        initial_state=HydroState.zeros(n_nodes, device=device),
        graph=graph,
        node_coords=hydro["node_coords"],
        territorial=territorial,
        withdrawals=withdrawals,
        day_of_year=day_of_year[:spinup_end],
    )
    h_ctx = model._last_h_context  # GRU hidden state from spinup
    print(f"Spinup done (0:{spinup_end})")

    # Phase 2: simulation continue spinup_end -> val_end
    Q_all, _ = model.simulate(
        forcing=forcing[spinup_end:val_end_idx],
        initial_state=spun_state,
        graph=graph,
        node_coords=hydro["node_coords"],
        territorial=territorial,
        withdrawals=withdrawals,
        day_of_year=day_of_year[spinup_end:val_end_idx],
        h_context=h_ctx,
    )
    print(f"Full simulation done ({spinup_end}:{val_end_idx})")

    # Extraire uniquement la partie validation
    val_offset = val_start_idx - spinup_end
    Q_sim = Q_all[val_offset:]
    del Q_all
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

print(f"Q_sim shape (val only): {Q_sim.shape}")
```

## KGE par station

```{python}
from meandre.utils.metrics import kge as kge_fn

# Build station_mask and q_obs from obs dict (same as slso.py)
station_node_map = obs["station_node_map"]   # {station_id: node_idx}
station_indices = sorted(set(station_node_map.values()))
n_stations = len(station_indices)

station_mask = torch.zeros(n_nodes, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
for ni in station_indices:
    station_mask[ni] = True

discharge_np = obs["discharge"]  # (T, N_all)
q_obs_val = torch.from_numpy(discharge_np[val_start_idx:val_end_idx, station_indices]).to(device)
q_sim_sta = Q_sim[:, station_mask]

# Station info from DuckDB
import duckdb
con = duckdb.connect(cfg["paths"]["basin_db"], read_only=True)
sta_df = con.execute("SELECT station_id, node_idx, drainage_area_km2 FROM stations ORDER BY node_idx").df()
con.close()

node_to_info = {row["node_idx"]: row for _, row in sta_df.iterrows()}
# Reverse map: node_idx -> station_id
idx_to_sid = {v: k for k, v in station_node_map.items()}

results = []
for s in range(n_stations):
    q_o = q_obs_val[:, s]
    q_s = q_sim_sta[:, s]
    valid = ~torch.isnan(q_o) & ~torch.isnan(q_s)
    if valid.sum() < 30:
        continue
    kge_val = float(kge_fn(q_o[valid], q_s[valid]))
    node_i = station_indices[s]
    info = node_to_info.get(node_i, {})
    results.append({
        "station_id": idx_to_sid.get(node_i, f"node_{node_i}"),
        "node_idx": node_i,
        "drainage_area_km2": info.get("drainage_area_km2", np.nan),
        "kge": kge_val,
        "n_valid": int(valid.sum()),
    })

df = pd.DataFrame(results)
df["log_area"] = np.log10(df["drainage_area_km2"])

# Ajouter les coordonnées des stations
sta_coords = {}
con2 = duckdb.connect(cfg["paths"]["basin_db"], read_only=True)
for _, row in con2.execute("SELECT station_id, lon, lat FROM stations").df().iterrows():
    sta_coords[row["station_id"]] = (row["lon"], row["lat"])
con2.close()

df["lon"] = df["station_id"].map(lambda s: sta_coords.get(s, (np.nan, np.nan))[0])
df["lat"] = df["station_id"].map(lambda s: sta_coords.get(s, (np.nan, np.nan))[1])
df["prefix"] = df["station_id"].str[:3]
df["kge_cat"] = pd.cut(df["kge"], bins=[-10, 0, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.8, 1.0],
                        labels=["<0", "0-0.3", "0.3-0.5", "0.5-0.7", "0.7-0.8", ">0.8"])

print(f"{len(df)} stations avec KGE calculable")
print(f"\nKGE par prefixe de station:")
print(df.groupby("prefix")["kge"].agg(["count", "median", "min", "max"]).to_string())
df.sort_values("kge").head(10)
```

## Scatter plot

```{python}
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html()

p = (
    ggplot(df, aes(x="log_area", y="kge", label="station_id"))
    + geom_point(size=3)
    + geom_text(nudge_y=0.03, size=7)
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0.8, linetype="dashed")
    + geom_hline(yintercept=float(df["kge"].median()), linetype="dotted")
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", se=True)
    + labs(
        x="log10(Superficie de drainage, km²)",
        y="KGE (validation)",
        title="KGE par station vs superficie de drainage",
        subtitle=f"Médiane KGE = {df['kge'].median():.3f}, n = {len(df)} stations",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)
p
```

## Carte spatiale des KGE

```{python}
p_map = (
    ggplot(df, aes(x="lon", y="lat", color="kge", size="drainage_area_km2", label="station_id"))
    + geom_point()
    + geom_text(aes(color="kge"), nudge_y=0.04, size=7)
    + scale_color_gradient2(low="red", mid="orange", high="blue", midpoint=0.5)
    + scale_size(range=[3, 10])
    + labs(
        x="Longitude", y="Latitude",
        title="KGE par station (validation 2023-2024)",
        subtitle=f"Médiane KGE = {df['kge'].median():.3f}",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + coord_fixed(ratio=1.3)
)
p_map
```

## Corrélation et diagnostic

```{python}
from scipy.stats import spearmanr
rho, pval = spearmanr(df["log_area"], df["kge"])
print(f"Spearman rho(log_area, kge) = {rho:.3f}, p = {pval:.4f}")

rho_lon, pval_lon = spearmanr(df["lon"], df["kge"])
rho_lat, pval_lat = spearmanr(df["lat"], df["kge"])
print(f"Spearman rho(lon, kge) = {rho_lon:.3f}, p = {pval_lon:.4f}")
print(f"Spearman rho(lat, kge) = {rho_lat:.3f}, p = {pval_lat:.4f}")

print(f"\nStations sous KGE=0.5:")
print(df[df["kge"] < 0.5][["station_id", "prefix", "drainage_area_km2", "lon", "lat", "kge"]].sort_values("kge").to_string(index=False))
```


## Variabilité spatiale des paramètres NeRF

```{python}
# Évaluer les paramètres NeRF sur tous les nœuds
with torch.no_grad():
    sp = model.spatial_encoder(
        hydro["node_coords"],
        territorial.to_tensor(),
    )

# Construire un DataFrame des paramètres clés par noeud
coords_np = hydro["node_coords"].cpu().numpy()
param_names = ["K_sat_1", "K_sat_2", "porosity_1", "C_f", "T_melt",
               "manning_n", "krec", "k_gw", "slope_factor", "frost_alpha"]

param_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "lon": coords_np[:, 0],
    "lat": coords_np[:, 1],
})
for pname in param_names:
    param_df[pname] = getattr(sp, pname).cpu().numpy()

# Stats nord vs sud (seuil lat=46.3)
param_df["region"] = np.where(param_df["lat"] > 46.3, "Nord (>46.3)", "Sud (<=46.3)")
print("Paramètres NeRF par région (médiane):")
print(param_df.groupby("region")[param_names].median().T.to_string())
print(f"\nNœuds: Nord={( param_df['region'] == 'Nord (>46.3)').sum()}, Sud={(param_df['region'] == 'Sud (<=46.3)').sum()}")
```

```{python}
# Cartes spatiales des paramètres clés (K_sat_1, C_f, krec, manning_n)
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html()

key_params = ["K_sat_1", "C_f", "krec", "manning_n"]
plots = []
for pname in key_params:
    p = (
        ggplot(param_df, aes(x="lon", y="lat", color=pname))
        + geom_point(size=0.5, alpha=0.7)
        + scale_color_gradient(low="yellow", high="darkblue")
        + labs(title=pname)
        + theme_minimal()
        + coord_fixed(ratio=1.3)
    )
    plots.append(p)

# 2x2 grid via gggrid
from lets_plot import gggrid
gggrid(plots, ncol=2)
```

```{python}
# Coefficient de variation spatial (CV) — mesure si le NeRF différencie les nœuds
print("Coefficient de variation spatial des paramètres NeRF:")
for pname in param_names:
    vals = param_df[pname]
    cv = vals.std() / (vals.mean() + 1e-10)
    print(f"  {pname:20s}  mean={vals.mean():.4f}  std={vals.std():.4f}  CV={cv:.3f}")
```
